title: Addressing Food Fraud in Gelatin Products with qPCR Verification
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Gelatin,   widely     used   in   food  ,   pharmaceuticals  ,   and     cosmetics  ,   is     vulnerable     to     adulteration     due     to     its     high     economic     value  .   Fraudulent     substitution     of     porcine  ,   bovine  ,   or     fish-derived   gelatin   threatens     religious     compliance  ,   consumer     trust  ,   and     international     trade  . Real-  time   PCR (  qPCR  ) has   emerged   as   a     powerful     tool     to     authenticate   gelatin   sources     by     detecting     species-specific   DNA   fragments  ,   even   in   highly     processed     products  .   Its     rapid     turnaround     time  ,   high     specificity  ,   and     sensitivity     make     qPCR   superior   to     conventional     methods     like   ELISA   or     chromatography  .   Integrating     qPCR-based     authentication     into     national     food     safety     and     quality     assurance     programs     will     ensure     traceability  ,   protect     consumer     rights  ,   and     support     compliance     with   halal,   kosher  ,   and     regulatory     standards  ,   ultimately     strengthening     both     domestic     and     export     market     confidence  .
steps_raw: Gelatin,   widely     used   in   food  ,   pharmaceuticals  ,   and     cosmetics  ,   is     vulnerable     to     adulteration     due     to     its     high     economic     value  .   Fraudulent     substitution     of     porcine  ,   bovine  ,   or     fish-derived   gelatin   threatens     religious     compliance  ,   consumer     trust  ,   and     international     trade  . Real-  time   PCR (  qPCR  ) has   emerged   as   a     powerful     tool     to     authenticate   gelatin   sources     by     detecting     species-specific   DNA   fragments  ,   even   in   highly     processed     products  .   Its     rapid     turnaround     time  ,   high     specificity  ,   and     sensitivity     make     qPCR   superior   to     conventional     methods     like   ELISA   or     chromatography  .   Integrating     qPCR-based     authentication     into     national     food     safety     and     quality     assurance     programs     will     ensure     traceability  ,   protect     consumer     rights  ,   and     support     compliance     with   halal,   kosher  ,   and     regulatory     standards  ,   ultimately     strengthening     both     domestic     and     export     market     confidence  .
